title: Abbreviations of Vaccines and Other Immunizing Agents | Vaccines & Immunizations | CDC
text:
and Other Immunizing Agents 
July 29, 2025 
At a glance 
Access an alphabetical list of current and old abbreviations used for vaccines on U.S. records.
A – G 
aIIV3 
Standard-dose, egg-based inactivated influenza vaccine with MF59
adjuvant, trivalent (Fluad)
aIIV4 
Standard-dose, egg-based inactivated influenza vaccine with MF59
adjuvant, quadrivalent (Fluad Quadrivalent)
AVA 
Anthrax vaccine adsorbed (Biothrax, Cyfendus)
BCG 
Bacille Calmette-Guérin vaccine (BCG, Tice BCG)
ccIIV3 
Standard-dose, cell culture-based inactivated influenza vaccine, trivalent
(Flucelvax)
ccIIV4 
Standard-dose, cell culture-based inactivated
influenza vaccine, quadrivalent (Flucelvax Quadrivalent)
CHIK-LA 
Live, attenuated chikungunya 
vaccine (Ixchiq)
CHIK-VLP 
Recombinant, virus-like
particle chikungunya vaccine (Vimkunya)
1vCOV-mRNA 
Monovalent mRNA COVID-19
vaccine (Comirnaty/PfizerBioNTech COVID-19 vaccine, Spikevax/Moderna COVID-19 vaccine)
2vCOV-mRNA 
Bivalent mRNA COVID-19
vaccine (Pfizer-BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine, Moderna COVID-19 vaccine)
1vCOV-aPS 
Monovalent protein
subunit COVID-19 vaccine, Adjuvanted (Nuvaxovid/Novavax COVID-19 vaccine)
CVD 103-HgR 
Live, attenuated
oral cholera vaccine (Vaxchora)
DEN4CYD 
Dengue vaccine (Dengvaxia)
DPT 
Replaced by the term DTP (see DTP for description)
DT 
Diphtheria and tetanus toxoids (no longer
available) 
DTaP 
Diphtheria and tetan
